info.title: zapo-rest API
info.description:
# zapo-rest

REST API multi-session para WhatsApp baseada em [zapo-js](https://zapo.to).

## Autenticação

| Tipo | Origem | Escopo |
|------|--------|--------|
| **Admin** | env `ADMIN_API_KEY` | Todas as instâncias + create/delete/rotate |
| **Instance** | campo `apiKey` da instância (plaintext) | Somente a instância dona da key |

Envie em **todas** as rotas `/v1/*`:

```http
X-Api-Key: <sua-chave>
```

ou

```http
Authorization: Bearer <sua-chave>
```

`GET /health` e `GET /ready` são públicos.

## Fluxo rápido (instância)

```bash
# 1) Criar (admin)
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"sales-1","webhookUrl":"https://example.com/webhooks/zapo","webhookEvents":["instance.qr","instance.connection","message.inbound","call.incoming"]}'

# 2) Conectar
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/connect" -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"

# 3) QR
curl -s "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/qr" -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"

# 4) Enviar texto (admin ou instance key)
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/messages/text" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","text":"Olá! Mensagem enviada via zapo-rest 👋","linkPreview":true}'
```

## Webhooks

Payload envelope:

```json
{
  "event": "message.inbound",
  "instance": "sales-1",
  "timestamp": "2026-07-11T12:10:00.000Z",
  "data": {
    "key": {
      "remoteJid": "5511888888888@s.whatsapp.net",
      "fromMe": false,
      "id": "ABC123"
    },
    "message": {
      "conversation": "Oi"
    },
    "pushName": "Cliente"
  }
}
```

## VoIP (áudio live)

Após `POST.../calls`, abra o WebSocket:

```
ws(s)://<host>/v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=<key>
```

- Server → JSON `{ "op": "ready", "sampleRate": 16000, "format": "f32le" }`
- Client ↔ server: frames **binários** Float32 LE mono @ 16 kHz
- Backpressure: JSON `{ "op": "backpressure", "pause": true | false }`

## Erros

Todas as falhas usam:

```json
{ "error": { "code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "…", "details": {} } }
```

Códigos comuns: `UNAUTHORIZED`, `FORBIDDEN`, `NOT_FOUND`, `CONFLICT`, `VALIDATION_ERROR`, `SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`, `BAD_REQUEST`.

## OpenAPI

- **UI (Scalar):** [`/docs`](/docs)
- **JSON:** [`/docs/json`](/docs/json) — gerado automaticamente a partir dos schemas Zod das rotas (`fastify-type-provider-zod` + `@fastify/swagger`)

## Guia rico

Documentação narrativa (arquitetura, fluxos, exemplos): **[/guide](/guide/)**.

apiKey.name: X-Api-Key
apiKey.description: Admin (`ADMIN_API_KEY`) or instance `apiKey`. Preferred authentication method.

bearerAuth.description: Same key as X-Api-Key, sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

get.description:
Returns `200 { "status": "ok" }` when the process is up. **No authentication.**

Use for load balancers / Kubernetes liveness probes.

Checks Postgres connectivity with `SELECT 1`.

- `200 { "status": "ready" }` when the database is reachable
- `503 { "status": "not_ready" }` otherwise

**No authentication.** Use for readiness gates before sending traffic.

Identifies whether the provided API key is **admin** or an **instance** key.

Used by the dashboard after login.

**Responses:**
- Admin: `{ "role": "admin" }`
- Instance: `{ "role": "instance", "instance": { ...full instance including apiKey } }`

**Example**
```bash
curl -s "$BASE/v1/me" -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

status.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle: `created` registered but not connected; `connecting` socket/handshake in progress; `qr` waiting for QR scan; `pairing` pairing-code flow; `open` ready to send/receive; `close` disconnected (credentials kept); `logged_out` device unlinked — re-pair required.

pairPhone.description: Optional phone hint for pairing-code flows

lastQr.description: Raw QR payload from last `auth_qr` event — render as QR image on clients

lastQrAt.description: ISO-8601 time when lastQr was updated

instance.description: WhatsApp multi-session instance (read view — API key omitted)

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

post.description:
**Admin only.** Provisions a new WhatsApp session.

- Generates a unique **instance API key**, returned **once** in this response (stored hashed — save it now)
- Does **not** open the socket yet — call `POST .../connect` next
- `name` becomes the zapo `sessionId` (stable across restarts)

**Example body**
```json
{
  "name": "sales-1",
  "webhookUrl": "https://example.com/webhooks/zapo",
  "webhookEvents": [
    "instance.qr",
    "instance.connection",
    "message.inbound",
    "call.incoming"
  ]
}
```

**Example**
```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"sales-1","webhookUrl":"https://example.com/webhooks/zapo","webhookEvents":["instance.qr","instance.connection","message.inbound","call.incoming"]}'
```

webhookUrl.description: Optional HTTPS webhook URL for events

webhookEvents.description: Optional event filter; omit or empty for all events

pairPhone.description: Optional phone digits for later pairing-code use

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

status.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle: `created` registered but not connected; `connecting` socket/handshake in progress; `qr` waiting for QR scan; `pairing` pairing-code flow; `open` ready to send/receive; `close` disconnected (credentials kept); `logged_out` device unlinked — re-pair required.

pairPhone.description: Optional phone hint for pairing-code flows

lastQr.description: Raw QR payload from last `auth_qr` event — render as QR image on clients

lastQrAt.description: ISO-8601 time when lastQr was updated

apiKey.description: Instance API key in plaintext. Returned only on create/rotate and shown once — store it immediately. Scope: this instance only. Header `X-Api-Key` or `Authorization: Bearer`.

instance.description: WhatsApp instance plus its new API key (create / rotate response only)

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

get.description:
**Admin only.** Returns every instance with full metadata. The **`apiKey` is not included** — it is only shown once at create/rotate. Use `POST .../keys/rotate` to mint a new one.

```bash
curl -s "$BASE/v1/instances" -H "X-Api-Key: $ADMIN_API_KEY"
```

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

status.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle: `created` registered but not connected; `connecting` socket/handshake in progress; `qr` waiting for QR scan; `pairing` pairing-code flow; `open` ready to send/receive; `close` disconnected (credentials kept); `logged_out` device unlinked — re-pair required.

pairPhone.description: Optional phone hint for pairing-code flows

lastQr.description: Raw QR payload from last `auth_qr` event — render as QR image on clients

lastQrAt.description: ISO-8601 time when lastQr was updated

items.description: WhatsApp multi-session instance (read view — API key omitted)

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

get.description:
Returns one instance. The **`apiKey` is not included** (shown only once at create/rotate).

- **Admin** may read any instance
- **Instance key** may only read its own `name` (otherwise `403`)

```bash
curl -s "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1" -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

delete.description:
**Admin only.** Unlinks the companion device when possible (`logout`), stops the client, and deletes metadata.

Irreversible without re-pairing. Prefer `disconnect` if you only want to stop the process temporarily.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

post.description:
Opens the WhatsApp Web socket for the instance (zapo `client.connect()`).

- First time: emits QR (`status: qr`) or pairing flow
- After pairing: resumes from stored credentials (`status: open`)
- Spawns reconnect-with-backoff on transient disconnects

Poll `GET .../qr` or listen to webhook `instance.qr` while pairing.

```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/connect" -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

webhookUrl.description: Webhook URL receiving JSON events, or null

webhookEvents.description: Event allow-list. Empty = all. Examples: `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`. `*` = all.

post.description:
Gracefully closes the socket **without** unlinking the device (`client.disconnect()`).

Credentials remain in the zapo store — next `connect` resumes without a new QR.

Do **not** confuse with `DELETE` (logout + remove).

get.description:
Returns the last cached QR string from event `auth_qr`.

- Render `qr` as a QR **image** (dashboard does this automatically)
- `null` when already paired or not in QR state
- WhatsApp rotates QR; keep polling (~2–3s) or use webhook `instance.qr`

```bash
curl -s "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/qr" -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

qr.description: Raw QR string to encode as image; null if none cached

expiresAt.description: When the QR was last received (server rotates QR)

status.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle: `created` registered but not connected; `connecting` socket/handshake in progress; `qr` waiting for QR scan; `pairing` pairing-code flow; `open` ready to send/receive; `close` disconnected (credentials kept); `logged_out` device unlinked — re-pair required.

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

post.description:
Requests an 8-character pairing code (`client.auth.requestPairingCode`).

**Prerequisites:** instance must be **connected** and in a pairing-capable state.
On the phone: WhatsApp → Linked devices → **Link with phone number instead**.

**Example body**
```json
{ "phone": "5511999999999" }
```

phone.description: Phone with country code (e.g. `5511999999999`). Non-digits are stripped.

schema.description: Request 8-char pairing code (Linked devices → Link with phone number)

requestBody.description: Request 8-char pairing code (Linked devices → Link with phone number)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

code.description: 8-character pairing code from WhatsApp
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

post.description:
**Admin only.** Generates a new instance `apiKey`, invalidates the previous one, and returns the instance with the new key **shown once**.

Update all integrations immediately after rotation.

get.summary: Live resource snapshot for instance / process
get.description: Process memory & CPU (Node process), live session share estimate, and storage usage for this instance. CPU/memory are process-wide (multi-session); heap is split equally among live sessions when possible.

parameters.name: name

post.description:
Sends a plain-text WhatsApp message via `client.message.send`.

**Requirements:** instance connected (`status: open`).

**Example body**
```json
{
  "to": "5511999999999",
  "text": "Olá! Mensagem enviada via zapo-rest 👋",
  "linkPreview": true
}
```

to.description: Recipient: digits (`5511999999999`), PN JID (`…@s.whatsapp.net`), group (`…@g.us`), or LID (`…@lid`)

linkPreview.description: Force/disable link preview for URLs in text

mentions.description: JIDs to mention in groups; include `@number` tokens in text

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

code.description: Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)
Machine-readable error code (UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, NOT_FOUND, VALIDATION_ERROR, …)

Example: `5568981159096` → `556881159096@s.whatsapp.net`

post.summary: Resolve correct WhatsApp JID for numbers

Like `POST /chat/whatsappNumbers`:
- expands BR **nono dígito** / MX-AR variants

- caches results (Redis/memory) for 24h

get.summary: Get profile picture (durable storage + TTL revalidation)
get.description:
Returns contact avatar with **bytes in object storage** (deterministic key, overwrite on change).

- Within TTL: serve our stored file **without** hitting WhatsApp.
- After TTL (or `refresh=true`): revalidate via IQ; compare `id`/sha256; download+overwrite only if changed.
- Privacy / no pic: delete stored object (no orphans).
- Binary stream: `GET.../profile-picture/file`.
Do not spam `refresh` (WhatsApp rate-overlimit).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: preview = compact; image = high resolution

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Force refresh from WhatsApp (default false; cache 24h)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Instance name

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone digits or JID of the contact

picture.description: Avatar payload; `url` points to our durable storage (or API file path), not ephemeral WA CDN

jid.description: Resolved contact JID used for the query

reason.description: When picture is null: not-authorized | item-not-found | privacy | …

cacheTtlSeconds.description: Revalidation TTL (seconds); default 86400 — controls WA IQ, not file lifetime

post.description:
Sends a chat-state into a conversation:

| state | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `composing` | Typing… |
| `recording` | Recording voice note |
| `paused` | Stopped |

Path param `jid` may be digits or a full JID (URL-encode `@` as `%40`).

```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/chats/5511999999999/chatstate" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "state": "composing" }'
```

post.description:
Places an **audio-only** WhatsApp voice call (`client.voip.startCall`).

Returns `callId` immediately after the offer is sent. Progress continues via webhooks `call.state` / `call.ended` and the live PCM WebSocket.

**No file / audioUrl playback** — open the stream and send live mic PCM.

**Example body**
```json
{
  "to": "5511888888888"
}
```

```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/calls" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511888888888"}'
```

post.summary: Audio blast — call + play predefined audio + record response
post.description:
Outbound VoIP **audio blast**: dial → wait for answer → play WAV → optional remote-leg record + Whisper STT.

### Audio
- **WAV only** (PCM 8/16/24/32-bit or float32). Any rate/channels → resampled to **16 kHz mono**.
- `audioUrl` is fetched server-side with **SSRF protection** (public HTTPS only, no redirects, size/time caps).

### Recording & STT
- With `recordResponse` (default true) the remote PCM is stored and linked on the call row — `GET .../calls/{callId}/recording` and `POST .../transcribe` work afterwards.
- Transcription runs when `transcribe` is not false **and** `STT_ENABLED` + `STT_API_URL` + `STT_API_KEY` are set (Groq Whisper recommended).

### Timeouts
The HTTP request stays open until the blast finishes. Raise your client timeout for long WAVs (`callTimeoutMs` + audio duration + `responseTimeoutMs`).

**Example**
```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/calls/blast" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","audioUrl":"https://cdn.example.com/prompt.wav","responseTimeoutMs":5000,"recordResponse":true,"transcribe":true,"sttLanguage":"pt"}'
```

audioUrl.description: HTTPS URL of a WAV file (PCM 8/16/24/32-bit or float32; any sample rate/channels — resampled to 16 kHz mono). Server-side download is **SSRF-guarded** (public hosts only, no redirects, size/time caps). Prefer hosting on your CDN.

responseTimeoutMs.description: Time to wait for a spoken response after the audio finishes (ms)

callTimeoutMs.description: Max time to wait for the callee to answer (ms)

recordResponse.description: Record the remote audio leg. Requires media storage. Persists on the call row so `GET .../recording` and `POST .../transcribe` work.

transcribe.description: When true (default if omitted) and `STT_ENABLED` + `STT_API_URL` + `STT_API_KEY` are set, run Whisper STT on the recorded response. Set false to skip.

sttLanguage.description: Language hint for STT (ISO 639-1, e.g. `pt`). Overrides `STT_LANGUAGE` env.

schema.description: Outbound VoIP **audio blast**: dial, play a WAV when answered, optionally record the remote leg and transcribe via Groq/OpenAI-compatible Whisper. HTTP stays open until the blast finishes — raise client timeouts for long WAVs. Env: `STT_*` for transcription.

requestBody.description: Outbound VoIP **audio blast**: dial, play a WAV when answered, optionally record the remote leg and transcribe via Groq/OpenAI-compatible Whisper. HTTP stays open until the blast finishes — raise client timeouts for long WAVs. Env: `STT_*` for transcription.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique instance name / zapo sessionId (e.g. `sales-1`). Alphanumeric, `_` and `-` only.

audioPlayed.description: True only if the full WAV was fed to the live buffer (false if aborted mid-play or not answered)

recordingUrl.description: URL for the stored remote-leg WAV when recording succeeded (`GET .../calls/{callId}/recording` or storage URL). Null if not recorded.

transcription.description: STT text when transcription ran successfully
transcription.example: Olá, tudo bem? Gostaria de confirmar...

example.transcription: Olá, tudo bem? Gostaria de confirmar minha consulta.

post.summary: Transcribe a call recording (STT via Groq / OpenAI-compatible)
post.description:
Runs speech-to-text on a **stored call recording** (`recordingStatus=ready`) via a Groq/OpenAI-compatible API.

### Env
| Var | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `STT_ENABLED` | must be `true` |
| `STT_API_URL` | base URL, e.g. `https://api.groq.com/openai` |
| `STT_API_KEY` | Bearer token |
| `STT_MODEL` | default `whisper-large-v3` |
| `STT_LANGUAGE` | optional ISO 639-1 hint |

Recordings come from **audio blast** (`POST .../calls/blast`) or softphone call-recording.

**Example**
```bash
curl -s -X POST "$BASE/v1/instances/sales-1/calls/$CALL_ID/transcribe" \
  -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY"
```

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Instance name

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Call id from start or webhook

200.description: Speech-to-text over an existing call recording (`recordingStatus=ready`). Requires `STT_ENABLED`, `STT_API_URL`, `STT_API_KEY`.

schema.description: Speech-to-text over an existing call recording (`recordingStatus=ready`). Requires `STT_ENABLED`, `STT_API_URL`, `STT_API_KEY`.

example.text: Olá, tudo bem? Eu gostaria de confirmar minha consulta.

get.summary: Download media for a message (original filename)
get.description:
Authorizes access, ensures the object exists in storage, then **redirects (302)** to storage
when possible (default) so file bytes do not transit the API.

If the CAS object was deleted from storage, the API **re-downloads from WhatsApp** (using
the stored message `raw`), re-uploads to storage, then redirects/streams. Only fails if
WhatsApp can no longer provide the media.

- **S3/MinIO:** presigned GET with original filename (ResponseContentDisposition).
- **`?proxy=1`:** stream through the API (no redirect).
- **`?download=1`:** attachment disposition.

get.description:
Unidirectional live event stream (messages, connection, presence, calls, …).

**Auth: put the API key in headers**, not in the URL (avoids access logs, proxies, Referer).

### curl (recommended)
```bash
curl -N -H "X-Api-Key: $KEY" -H "Accept: text/event-stream" \
  "$BASE/v1/events?instance=sales-1"
```

### Browser (fetch + stream — can send headers)
```js
const res = await fetch(`/v1/events?instance=sales-1`, {
  headers: { "X-Api-Key": key, Accept: "text/event-stream" },
})
// read res.body with TextDecoder…
```

Native `EventSource` cannot set headers — only then use `?apiKey=` (discouraged).

- **Instance keys** are always scoped to their instance.
- **Admin** may omit `instance` (all) or filter with `instance=`.
- First event: `{ "event": "connected", "role", "instance", "timestamp" }`.
- Keepalive: SSE comments every 15s (`: ping …`).

**WebSocket upgrade** for bidirectional live VoIP audio (not a plain HTTP JSON response).

### URL
```
ws(s)://<host>/v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=<key>
```

Auth: query `apiKey` (browsers) and/or header `X-Api-Key`.

### Protocol
1. Server → JSON text: `{ "op": "ready", "sampleRate": 16000, "channels": 1, "format": "f32le", "callId": "..." }`
2. Client → server **binary**: Float32 LE mono PCM @ 16 kHz (microphone)
3. Server → client **binary**: same format (peer audio)
4. Backpressure JSON: `{ "op": "backpressure", "pause": true | false, "bufferedMs": N }`
5. End: `{ "op": "ended", "callId": "..." }` then socket close

Uses `setExternalAudioMode` — **no file autoplay**. Multi-call supported up to `VOIP_MAX_CONCURRENT_CALLS`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Instance name

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Call id from POST.../calls or webhook call.incoming

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: API key for browser WebSocket clients

404.description: Instance or call not found (socket close 4404)

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Liveness and readiness probes (public — no API key).

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
Authentication uses **two key types**:

1. **Admin** — `ADMIN_API_KEY` from environment. Full access: create/list/delete instances, rotate keys, act on any instance.
2. **Instance** — per-instance `apiKey`, returned **once** on create/rotate (stored hashed, never shown again). Scoped to that instance only.

Send the key as:
- Header `X-Api-Key: <key>` **(preferred)**
- or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`

Dashboard login uses the same keys via `GET /v1/me`.

tags.name: Instances
tags.description:
Multi-session lifecycle. Each instance is one WhatsApp linked device (zapo `sessionId`).

**Typical flow:**
1. `POST /v1/instances` (admin) → receive `apiKey`
2. `POST /v1/instances/{name}/connect`
3. `GET /v1/instances/{name}/qr` → scan with WhatsApp → Linked devices
4. Status becomes `open` — send messages / receive webhooks

Alternatively use pairing code: `POST.../pairing-code` after connect when status is `pairing`/`qr`.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description:
Send WhatsApp messages through a connected instance (`status: open`).

Supports **text**, **image**, **audio** (incl. PTT), and **document**. Video is not exposed in this API.

`to` accepts digits with country code or full JID. Media: provide `mediaUrl` (HTTPS) or `mediaBase64`.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description:
Contact utilities: check if numbers exist on WhatsApp (LID sync) and fetch profile pictures.

Instance must be connected. Rate limits may apply on WhatsApp side for bulk checks.

tags.name: Presence
tags.description: Presence and chat-state (typing / recording). Chat-state is per conversation; presence is account-level.

tags.name: Calls
tags.description:
**Live VoIP audio only** (WhatsApp voice calls via `@zapo-js/voip`).

1. `POST.../calls` with `{ "to": "5511…" }` → `{ callId }`
2. Open WebSocket: `GET /v1/instances/{name}/calls/{callId}/stream?apiKey=...`
3. Exchange binary frames: **Float32 LE mono PCM @ 16 kHz**
4. Control: accept / reject / end / mute

No file autoplay when the peer answers — real phone-style streaming.
Multiple concurrent calls per instance up to `VOIP_MAX_CONCURRENT_CALLS`.

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: Per-instance analytics: message/call time series for charts, media storage breakdown, and process resource snapshots (memory/CPU are process-wide; storage is per-instance).

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
When `webhookUrl` is set on an instance, events are **HTTP POST** JSON:

```json
{
 "event": "message.inbound",
 "instance": "sales-1",
 "timestamp": "2026-07-11T12:10:00.000Z",
 "data": { }
}
```

**Events:** `instance.qr`, `instance.connection`, `instance.paired`, `instance.logged_out`, `message.inbound`, `call.incoming`, `call.state`, `call.ended`.

Filter with `webhookEvents` on create (empty = all). Delivery is best-effort with timeout `WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS`.
